title: 211info Earns Fourth Inform USA Accreditation for Excellence in Information & Referral Services - 211info
text: PORTLAND, Ore. – 211info has achieved Inform USA Accreditation, recognizing the organization’s commitment to delivering the highest standard of Information & Referral (I&R) services to communities across Oregon and Southwest Washington. The accreditation follows a comprehensive, third-party evaluation by Inform USA, the national leader in I&R standards. The process reviews all aspects of agency operations, including community partner feedback, database accuracy, call quality, and organizational effectiveness. 211info has now achieved accreditation for a fourth time, previously earning the distinction in 2006, 2012, 2019, and now 2025. The current accreditation is valid for a five-year period, with annual updates submitted to Inform USA to ensure ongoing compliance. During the review, the Inform USA Accreditation Commission commended 211info for: Exceptional referral database quality and accuracy: At the time of review, the database included 2,519 agencies, 4,455 sites, and 30,355 services, with 95.5% records verified as current. Inform USA noted, “This is remarkable, given the size of this database.” Strong quality assurance systems for performance monitoring and staff coaching. Well-established processes and planning for disaster, incident, and relief response. A unique collaborative program, the Pesticide Reporting Line, operated in partnership with the Oregon Department of Agriculture’s Pesticide Analytical Response Center (PARC). Inform USA Accreditation recognizes I&R organizations that meet rigorous national standards for quality, accuracy, and accountability. Accredited agencies demonstrate operational excellence, trusted information delivery, and strong community partnerships. 211info is proud to join a select group of Inform USA–accredited organizations nationwide .
